reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 4 (Exploitability): DISPUTED_FP - mitigated by downstream bounds checking. The finding claims ConstructDataColumnSidecars (reconstruction.go:150-166) does not validate that kzg.ComputeCells() returns exactly 128 cells, creating potential cells/proofs length mismatch in CellsAndProofs struct. However, this requires c-kzg-4844 library bug or version mismatch. The c-kzg-4844 library has a mathematical guarantee (per EIP-7594 spec) to return exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB (128) cells for any valid-sized blob. The code validates blob size at line 145 before calling ComputeCells. Downstream validation exists in das_core.go:dataColumnsSidecars() at lines 227-232, which explicitly checks `columnIndex >= len(cellsForRow)` and returns error if cells count is insufficient, catching the <128 case. The >128 case would use first 128 cells paired with 128 proofs, which is benign given correct library behavior. Missing validation is defensive programming best practice but not exploitable without library defect. No attacker can cause library misbehavior without memory corruption or malicious library substitution (out of scope). 01e reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json lines 679-696, invariant 'Each blob must produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs'. Verdict: Library correctness assumption is reasonable for standard cryptographic library; partial mitigation exists; not attacker-triggered.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Each blob must produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs in cells_and_kzg_proofs construction.
